text: I feel a sense of curious anticipation stepping into this bright, modern space punctuated by geometric design and neon lights. As I approach the exhibit, "R+D," the words and technology displayed inspire philosophical wonder about human innovation and connection. The environment feels both stimulating and slightly disorienting, as if inviting me to reflect on the boundaries of perception and the ways we observe—and are observed by—the world around us. A slight unease lingers beneath my curiosity, perhaps the hum of surveillance or the electric awareness of being part of an experiment in observation.
